decisions.briefing: Markets are neutral today with NASDAQ down 0.8% and S&P 500 flat — this is not the risk-on environment needed to justify new entries. Despite having 4 open slots and $577 in cash (just above the $500 threshold), strategy memory is explicit: do not force buys on neutral days just to deploy capital. All 6 existing positions are held; stop-losses and profit targets remain active and will handle exits automatically.

market_education.summary_en: U.S. equities were mixed on July 3rd, a holiday-shortened half-day session ahead of Independence Day, with the S&P 500 finishing flat and the NASDAQ slipping 0.8%. Tech and growth names faced light selling pressure as traders reduced exposure ahead of the long weekend, with thin volume amplifying intraday moves. No major macro catalyst drove the session — it was largely pre-holiday positioning and cautious risk management into the close.
market_education.summary_zh: 美股於7月3日（獨立日前夕半日交易）表現參差，標普500收平，納斯達克則下跌0.8%。科技及成長股於長週末前夕承受輕度沽壓，成交淡靜更放大了市場波幅。整個交易日缺乏重大宏觀催化劑，市況主要受假期前投資者主動降低持倉及風險管理情緒主導。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Markets often see lighter volume and directional drift in the days before a major holiday, as institutional traders reduce risk exposure rather than hold positions over a long weekend. Today's flat S&P 500 and mild NASDAQ dip on a half-day session is a textbook example — low conviction moves in either direction, making it a poor time to initiate new positions that lack a strong catalyst.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 「假期效應」是指在重大假日前夕，市場成交量萎縮、走勢偏向單邊漂移的現象。機構投資者傾向於在長週末前減少持倉，避免隔夜風險。今日標普500收平、納斯達克溫和回落，正是這一效應的典型呈現——市場缺乏方向性動力，雙向信念不足，並非建立新倉的好時機。
